title: Personal Portfolio Website for Boby Dhorajiya - Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Create Hero section on Home page with headline, subheadline, and CTA button
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section is visible at the top of the Home page
Headline, subheadline, and CTA button text match specifications exactly
CTA button is visually prominent and interactive

file_operations.description: Create Home page component with Hero section displaying headline 'Hi, I'm Boby — Mobile App Developer', subheadline 'I build secure, scalable, and high-performance mobile apps using Flutter & React Native.', and CTA button 'Let's Build Your App'. Use the generated hero background image at frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png as the section background. Wire the CTA button to navigate to the Contact page.

requirements.summary: Create About page displaying bio and positioning as Freelance Mobile App Developer
acceptanceCriteria: About page is accessible via navigation
Bio text matches specification
Freelance positioning is communicated clearly

file_operations.description: Create About page component displaying the bio text: 'Boby is a mobile-focused developer specializing in Flutter and React Native, with strong expertise in building secure and scalable applications for startups and enterprises.' Position him as a Freelance Mobile App Developer with appropriate headings and layout.

requirements.summary: Create Skills section displaying categorized skills
acceptanceCriteria: Skills are grouped by category
All listed skills are displayed
Layout is visually clear and readable on mobile and desktop

file_operations.description: Create Skills page component displaying categorized skills: Primary (Flutter, Dart), Secondary (React Native), Security (Secure API Integration, Authentication Security, Secure Local Storage, Data Encryption), and Additional (Firebase, REST APIs, Web Development). Use the generated security-shield-icon.dim_256x256.png from frontend/public/assets/generated/ for the Security category icon. Display skills in a responsive card grid layout grouped by category.

requirements.summary: Create Projects showcase page with project cards displaying key fields
acceptanceCriteria: Projects page is accessible via navigation
Each project card shows all six fields
At least one placeholder project is visible

file_operations.description: Create Projects page component that fetches projects from backend using getProjects(). Display each project as a card showing: App Name (title), Platform, Tech Used, Security Implemented, Description, and Role. Seed with placeholder data if no projects exist. Handle loading and error states appropriately.
Create custom hook useProjects that wraps useQuery to fetch projects from backend actor.getProjects(). Return projects data, loading state, and error state. Enable query only when actor is available.

requirements.summary: Create Services section with service cards
acceptanceCriteria: All seven services are displayed
Each service has a title and brief description
Layout is responsive

file_operations.description: Create Services page component that fetches services from backend using getServices(). Display service cards for: Flutter App Development, React Native Development, Secure Mobile App Architecture, API Integration, Firebase Integration, Mobile App Security Consulting, and Web Development Support. Use a responsive grid layout. Handle loading and error states.
Create custom hook useServices that wraps useQuery to fetch services from backend actor.getServices(). Return services data, loading state, and error state. Enable query only when actor is available.

requirements.summary: Create Experience page positioning Boby as Freelance Mobile App Developer
acceptanceCriteria: Experience page is accessible via navigation
Freelance role with Flutter & React Native focus is clearly stated

file_operations.description: Create Experience page component displaying Boby's experience as a Freelance Mobile App Developer working on production apps using Flutter and React Native. Include timeline or card layout with relevant experience details.

requirements.summary: Create Testimonials page that reads and displays testimonials from backend
acceptanceCriteria: Testimonials page fetches data from backend
Each testimonial shows name and message
Page is accessible via navigation

file_operations.description: Create Testimonials page component that fetches testimonials from backend using getTestimonials(). Display each testimonial as a card showing author name and message. Use a responsive layout. Handle loading and error states.

Create custom hook useTestimonials that wraps useQuery to fetch testimonials from backend actor.getTestimonials(). Return testimonials data, loading state, and error state. Enable query only when actor is available.

requirements.summary: Create Blog listing and detail pages with slug-based routing
acceptanceCriteria: Blog listing page shows post titles
Clicking a post navigates to a detail page via slug
Detail page shows full content

file_operations.description: Create Blog listing page component that fetches blog posts from backend using getBlogs(). Display each post as a card showing title, meta title, and meta description. Link each post to its detail page using slug-based routing (/blog/:slug). Handle loading and error states.

Create BlogPost detail page component that fetches a single blog post from backend using getBlogBySlug(slug). Display the full content including title and complete post content. Use useParams to extract the slug from the URL. Handle loading, error, and not-found states.
Create custom hook useBlogs that wraps useQuery to fetch all blog posts from backend actor.getBlogs(). Return blogs data, loading state, and error state. Enable query only when actor is available.

Create custom hook useBlogBySlug that accepts a slug parameter and wraps useQuery to fetch a single blog post from backend actor.getBlogBySlug(slug). Return blog data, loading state, and error state. Enable query only when actor is available and slug is provided.

requirements.summary: Create Contact page with form that saves leads to backend
acceptanceCriteria: Contact form has Name, Email, and Message fields
Successful submission stores the lead in the backend
User receives a confirmation message after submission

file_operations.description: Create Contact page component with a form containing Name, Email, and Message fields. On form submission, call backend processContactForm() method to save the lead. Display a success confirmation message after successful submission and handle error states with appropriate user feedback. Clear the form after successful submission.

Create custom hook useContactForm that wraps useMutation to submit contact form data to backend actor.processContactForm(name, email, message). Return mutation function, loading state, error state, and success state. Invalidate leads query on success if applicable.

requirements.summary: Create Admin Panel with Internet Identity authentication and CRUD management for all entities
acceptanceCriteria: Admin panel is only accessible after authentication via Internet Identity
Dashboard displays total Projects, Leads, and Blog post counts
Each managed entity has a list view and create/edit/delete functionality
Unauthenticated users are redirected to login

file_operations.description: Create Admin Dashboard page component that fetches and displays total counts for Projects, Leads, and Blog posts using getDashboardStats() from backend. Use the authorization component to ensure only authenticated admins can access this page. Display counts in a card grid layout with navigation links to each management section.

Create ProjectsManagement page component that lists all projects with create, edit, and delete functionality. Fetch projects using getProjects(). Use blob-storage component for project image uploads via ExternalBlob. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing. Include a form for creating/editing projects with fields: title, description, url, and image. Implement delete confirmation. Ensure admin-only access using authorization component.

Create BlogManagement page component that lists all blog posts with create, edit, and delete functionality. Fetch blogs using getBlogs(). Include a form for creating/editing blogs with fields: title, slug, metaTitle, metaDescription, and content. Implement delete confirmation. Ensure admin-only access using authorization component.

Create TestimonialsManagement page component that lists all testimonials with create, edit, and delete functionality. Fetch testimonials using getTestimonials(). Include a form for creating/editing testimonials with fields: author and message. Implement delete confirmation. Ensure admin-only access using authorization component.

Create SkillsManagement page component that lists all skills with create, edit, and delete functionality. Fetch skills using getSkills(). Include a form for creating/editing skills with fields: name and experience. Implement delete confirmation. Ensure admin-only access using authorization component.

Create ServicesManagement page component that lists all services with create, edit, and delete functionality. Fetch services using getServices(). Include a form for creating/editing services with fields: title and description. Implement delete confirmation. Ensure admin-only access using authorization component.

Create LeadsManagement page component that lists all contact form leads with view and delete functionality. Fetch leads using getLeads(). Display lead information including name, email, message, and timestamp. Implement delete confirmation. Ensure admin-only access using authorization component.

Create SeoManagement page component that lists all SEO settings with create, edit, and delete functionality per page. Fetch SEO settings using getSeoSettings(). Include a form for creating/editing SEO settings with fields: page, metaTitle, and metaDescription. Implement delete confirmation. Ensure admin-only access using authorization component.

Create AdminGuard wrapper component that uses the authorization component to verify admin access. If user is not authenticated, redirect to login. If authenticated but not an admin, show an access denied message. Use isCallerAdmin() from backend to verify admin role. Wrap all admin pages with this guard.

Create ProfileSetup modal component that prompts the user for their name and email when they log in for the first time. Use the authorization component's getCallerUserProfile() to check if profile exists and saveCallerUserProfile() to save the profile. Follow the component's usage instructions to prevent modal flash by checking both actor availability and profile fetch completion before showing the modal.

Create custom hook useAdminCheck that wraps useQuery to check if the current user is an admin using actor.isCallerAdmin(). Return isAdmin boolean, loading state, and error state. Enable query only when actor and identity are available.

Create custom hook useDashboardStats that wraps useQuery to fetch dashboard statistics from backend actor.getDashboardStats(). Return stats data (projectCount, leadCount, blogCount), loading state, and error state. Enable query only when actor is available.
Create custom hook useLeads that wraps useQuery to fetch leads from backend actor.getLeads(). Return leads data, loading state, and error state. Enable query only when actor is available.
Create custom hook useSkills that wraps useQuery to fetch skills from backend actor.getSkills(). Return skills data, loading state, and error state. Enable query only when actor is available.

Create custom hook useSeoSettings that wraps useQuery to fetch all SEO settings from backend actor.getSeoSettings(). Return SEO settings data, loading state, and error state. Enable query only when actor is available.

Create custom hook useProjectMutations that wraps useMutation for project CRUD operations: addProject, updateProject, deleteProject. Return mutation functions and states for each operation. Invalidate projects query on success. Handle ExternalBlob image uploads using the blob-storage component.

Create custom hook useBlogMutations that wraps useMutation for blog CRUD operations: addBlog, updateBlog, deleteBlog. Return mutation functions and states for each operation. Invalidate blogs query on success.

Create custom hook useTestimonialMutations that wraps useMutation for testimonial CRUD operations: addTestimonial, updateTestimonial, deleteTestimonial. Return mutation functions and states for each operation. Invalidate testimonials query on success.

Create custom hook useSkillMutations that wraps useMutation for skill CRUD operations: addSkill, updateSkill, deleteSkill. Return mutation functions and states for each operation. Invalidate skills query on success.

Create custom hook useServiceMutations that wraps useMutation for service CRUD operations: addService, updateService, deleteService. Return mutation functions and states for each operation. Invalidate services query on success.
Create custom hook useLeadMutations that wraps useMutation for lead delete operation: deleteLead. Return mutation function and state. Invalidate leads query on success.

Create custom hook useSeoMutations that wraps useMutation for SEO setting operations: setSeoSetting, deleteSeoSetting. Return mutation functions and states for each operation. Invalidate SEO settings query on success.

requirements.summary: Apply dark premium visual theme with mobile-first security-focused brand identity and dark/light mode toggle
acceptanceCriteria: Dark mode is the default with a toggle for light mode
Accent color is consistently applied across buttons, headings, and highlights
Animations are present on hero section and card entries
Layout is responsive and mobile-first

file_operations.description: Apply global dark theme styling with dark background (deep navy/black), high-contrast accent colors (electric cyan or green), clean modern typography, and smooth CSS transitions for hover and scroll effects. Define CSS custom properties for both dark and light themes. Ensure mobile-first responsive design principles are applied. Add subtle animations for fade-in, slide-in, and hover states using CSS transitions and keyframes.

Configure Tailwind theme to support dark/light mode with custom color palette including dark background (navy/black), accent colors (cyan, electric green), and extended animation utilities. Define custom spacing, typography, and breakpoints for mobile-first design. Ensure theme colors align with security-focused premium branding.

Create ThemeToggle component that switches between dark and light mode. Store theme preference in localStorage and apply the theme class to the document root. Default to dark mode on first visit. Display a sun/moon icon button in the navigation bar.

Create Layout component that wraps all pages with consistent header, footer, and theme application. Include navigation bar with all page links, theme toggle, and login/logout button. Apply dark theme classes to the root container. Ensure responsive layout with hamburger menu on mobile.

requirements.summary: Set per-page SEO meta titles and descriptions with admin update capability
acceptanceCriteria: Home page renders correct meta title and description
Admin panel allows editing SEO settings per page
Updated SEO settings are reflected on the respective page

file_operations.description: Create SEOHead component that accepts page name as prop, fetches SEO settings for that page using getSeoSettingByPage(), and renders meta tags for title and description in the document head using react-helmet-async or similar. Default to 'Boby Dhorajiya – Flutter & React Native Developer' and 'Mobile app developer specializing in Flutter, React Native & mobile security.' for Home page if no custom settings exist.

Create custom hook useSeoByPage that accepts a page name parameter and wraps useQuery to fetch SEO settings for that specific page from backend actor.getSeoSettingByPage(page). Return SEO setting data, loading state, and error state. Enable query only when actor is available and page name is provided.

requirements.summary: Add responsive top navigation bar with links to all pages and hamburger menu on mobile
acceptanceCriteria: All pages are reachable via the navigation bar
Navigation collapses to a hamburger menu on small screens
Active page is visually highlighted

file_operations.description: Create Navigation component with links to all pages: Home, About, Skills, Projects, Services, Experience, Testimonials, Blog, Contact, and Admin. Use React Router's NavLink for active page highlighting. Implement responsive design with hamburger menu on mobile screens using CSS and React state. Include ThemeToggle and LoginButton components in the navigation. Wire the navigation into the Layout component.

Create LoginButton component that uses the authorization component's useInternetIdentity hook to handle login/logout. Display 'Login' when not authenticated and 'Logout' when authenticated. On logout, clear query cache to remove all cached application data. Follow the component's usage instructions for proper authentication handling and error recovery.

Wire all created pages into React Router with the following routes: / (Home), /about (About), /skills (Skills), /projects (Projects), /services (Services), /experience (Experience), /testimonials (Testimonials), /blog (Blog listing), /blog/:slug (BlogPost detail), /contact (Contact), /admin (Admin Dashboard wrapped in AdminGuard), /admin/projects (ProjectsManagement), /admin/blog (BlogManagement), /admin/testimonials (TestimonialsManagement), /admin/skills (SkillsManagement), /admin/services (ServicesManagement), /admin/leads (LeadsManagement), /admin/seo (SeoManagement). Wrap all pages with Layout component. Add a 404 Not Found route. Ensure ProfileSetup modal is rendered at app root level and shown only when user is authenticated but has no profile.

requirements.summary: Generate and wire hero background image with dark futuristic digital security aesthetic
acceptanceCriteria: Hero background image is rendered in the Hero section
Image loads from frontend/public/assets/generated/
Image does not distract from hero text

file_operations.description: Update Home page Hero section to use the generated hero background image at frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png as a CSS background-image. Apply overlay gradient to ensure text readability and prevent distraction. Position the image to cover the hero section and ensure it loads efficiently.
